title: Advances in Computer Chess 3
text: Home * Conferences * Advances in Computer Chess 3 < Prev Next > Imperial College [1] The Advances in Computer Chess 3 conference was held at Imperial College , London , UK , on April 9-10, 1981 . The conference was planned and organized by Mike Clarke , who especially welcomed Mikhail Botvinnik at the opening ceremony [2] . Contents 1 Proceedings 2 Lectures 3 Reports 4 Forum Posts 5 External Links 6 References Proceedings [ edit ] Advances in Computer Chess 3 M.R.B. A Special-Purpose Machine for an Improved Search Algorithm for Deep Chess Combinations . Joe Condon , Ken Thompson ( 1982 ). Belle Chess Hardware . Ken Thompson ( 1982 ). Computer Chess Strength . Machine-Aided Refinement of Correct Strategies for the Endgame in Chess. Alen Shapiro , Tim Niblett ( 1982 ). Automatic Induction of Classification Rules for Chess End game. Thomas Nitsche ( 1982 ). A Learning Chess Program. Max Bramer , B. E. P. Alden ( 1982 ). A Program for Solving Retrograde Analysis Chess Problems. Donald Michie ( 1982 ). Information and Complexity in Chess .
